notes.content: The Rausser College of Natural Resources (RCNR) admits students directly into one of its nine undergraduate majors as selected by the applicant at the time of application. Because Rausser College does not admit transfer students as undeclared, each individual major has a distinct set of transfer courses required as major preparation before the applicant is minimally eligible for admission. Resources for RCNR transfer applicants: UC Berkeley Office of Undergraduate Admissions Minimum Requirements for Transfer Admission Rausser College of Natural Resources Transfer Applicants page - includes transfer admission guidelines specific to each Rausser College major, as well as additional information on how to submit a competitive application for your intended major Rausser College Major Requirements and Sample Degree Plans Rausser College Advising Staff
notes.contentHtml:
The Rausser College of Natural Resources (RCNR) admits students directly into one of its nine undergraduate majors as selected by the applicant at the time of application. Because Rausser College does not admit transfer students as undeclared, each individual major has a distinct set of transfer courses required as major preparation before the applicant is minimally eligible for admission.  


Resources for RCNR transfer applicants: 

  • UC Berkeley Office of Undergraduate Admissions Minimum Requirements for Transfer Admission
  • Rausser College of Natural Resources Transfer Applicants page - includes transfer admission guidelines specific to each Rausser College major, as well as additional information on how to submit a competitive application for your intended major 
  • Rausser College Major Requirements and Sample Degree Plans
  • Rausser College Advising Staff

notes.content: ABOUT THE CONSERVATION & RESOURCE STUDIES MAJOR The Conservation and Resource Studies (CRS) major is a self-designed interdisciplinary program for students interested in environmental issues and areas of interaction among natural resources, population, energy, technology, societal institutions, and cultural values. Students draw on the course offerings of the entire campus and appropriate community resources in the development of individual programs of study. The major’s orientation is toward flexibility and an individualized educational approach to understanding the structure and dynamic functions of complex environmental systems within our society and biosphere. It encourages interaction among students, faculty, and community. The major’s offerings are designed to help each student formulate an area of interest, but are not in any way meant to limit the range of options available. For additional information, please contact the Conservation & Resource Studies advisor at crs.emf.ugrad@berkeley.edu .
notes.contentHtml:
ABOUT THE CONSERVATION & RESOURCE STUDIES MAJOR


The Conservation and Resource Studies (CRS) major is a self-designed interdisciplinary program for students interested in environmental issues and areas of interaction among natural resources, population, energy, technology, societal institutions, and cultural values. Students draw on the course offerings of the entire campus and appropriate community resources in the development of individual programs of study. The major’s orientation is toward flexibility and an individualized educational approach to understanding the structure and dynamic functions of complex environmental systems within our society and biosphere. It encourages interaction among students, faculty, and community. The major’s offerings are designed to help each student formulate an area of interest, but are not in any way meant to limit the range of options available.


For additional information, please contact the Conservation & Resource Studies advisor at crs.emf.ugrad@berkeley.edu.

notes.content: Please Note: Applicants are expected to complete all required coursework by the end of the spring term prior to admission. Majors may allow one course pending in the summer term prior to admission if the major has more than five required courses and at least five courses of the required coursework have been completed; in this case, if a requirement includes both a lecture and lab, the lecture and lab together are treated as one course. Majors which require five or fewer required courses to transfer will not allow required coursework to be completed in the summer prior to admission. Please see the Rausser College of Natural Resources Transfer Applicants page for individual requirements by major. Conservation & Resource Studies applicants have five or fewer required courses to complete prior to transfer and will not be allowed to complete a required course in the summer prior to admission. To ensure full articulation of transfer coursework: if a series of courses at a community college is required (e.g., Chemistry 101 + 102 + 103 = CHEM 1A/1AL + CHEM 1B at UC Berkeley), all of the courses in the series must be completed, and must (unless otherwise indicated) be completed at the same community college. Partial completion of the series (e.g., two of the three required courses) may result in no credit toward the requirement(s), and splitting a series of courses between different community colleges is not guaranteed to satisfy the requirement. Courses taken out of series will be evaluated by college faculty during the transfer admissions process. If you have questions about the articulation of a series for your intended major, please email crs.emf.ugrad@berkeley.edu . Minimum Admissions Requirements in Conservation & Resource Studies: Equivalent of: READING & COMPOSITION A + READING & COMPOSTION B - satisfied by full UC IGETC, or may be satisfied with AP/IB/A-Level exam credit BIOLOGY 1A or BIOLOGY 1B or BIOLOGY 11 - may be satisfied with AP/IB/A-Level exam credit MATH 16A or MATH 51 or STAT 2 - may be satisfied with AP/IB/A-Level exam credit Highly Recommended (but not required) Prior to Transfer Transferable Social & Behavioral Sciences or International Studies breadth course - satisfied by full UC IGETC Transferable Physical Science breadth course - satisfied by full UC IGETC Transferable Arts & Literature, Historical Studies, or Philosophy & Values breadth course - satisfied by full UC IGETC Geography, environmental biology or ecology, chemistry or physics for upper division preparation depending on Area of Interest, environmental justice/law/policy
notes.contentHtml:
Please Note: Applicants are expected to complete all required coursework by the end of the spring term prior to admission. Majors may allow one course pending in the summer term prior to admission if the major has more than five required courses and at least five courses of the required coursework have been completed; in this case, if a requirement includes both a lecture and lab, the lecture and lab together are treated as one course. Majors which require five or fewer required courses to transfer will not allow required coursework to be completed in the summer prior to admission. Please see the Rausser College of Natural Resources Transfer Applicants page for individual requirements by major.


Conservation & Resource Studies applicants have five or fewer required courses to complete prior to transfer and will not be allowed to complete a required course in the summer prior to admission.



To ensure full articulation of transfer coursework: if a series of courses at a community college is required (e.g., Chemistry 101 + 102 + 103 = CHEM 1A/1AL + CHEM 1B at UC Berkeley), all of the courses in the series must be completed, and must (unless otherwise indicated) be completed at the same community college. Partial completion of the series (e.g., two of the three required courses) may result in no credit toward the requirement(s), and splitting a series of courses between different community colleges is not guaranteed to satisfy the requirement. Courses taken out of series will be evaluated by college faculty during the transfer admissions process. If you have questions about the articulation of a series for your intended major, please email crs.emf.ugrad@berkeley.edu.


Minimum Admissions Requirements in Conservation & Resource Studies:


Equivalent of: 

  • READING & COMPOSITION A + READING & COMPOSTION B - satisfied by full UC IGETC, or may be satisfied with AP/IB/A-Level exam credit
  • BIOLOGY 1A or BIOLOGY 1B or BIOLOGY 11 - may be satisfied with AP/IB/A-Level exam credit
  • MATH 16A or MATH 51 or STAT 2 - may be satisfied with AP/IB/A-Level exam credit



Highly Recommended (but not required) Prior to Transfer

  • Transferable Social & Behavioral Sciences or International Studies breadth course - satisfied by full UC IGETC
  • Transferable Physical Science breadth course - satisfied by full UC IGETC
  • Transferable Arts & Literature, Historical Studies, or Philosophy & Values breadth course - satisfied by full UC IGETC
  • Geography, environmental biology or ecology, chemistry or physics for upper division preparation depending on Area of Interest, environmental justice/law/policy
